Feb 26, 2026
Albert Einstein

Contact

Institute for Advanced Study
1 Einstein Drive
Princeton, NJ 08540
Phone: (609) 734-8000
einstein@ias.edu

Education

1905PhDPhysics, University of Zurich
1900DiplomaMathematics and Physics, Swiss Federal Polytechnic (ETH Zurich)

Professional Experience

1933-1955Professor of Theoretical PhysicsInstitute for Advanced Study, Princeton
1917-1933Director, Kaiser Wilhelm Institute for PhysicsKaiser Wilhelm Society, Berlin
1914-1917Professor of Theoretical PhysicsHumboldt University of Berlin
1912-1914Professor of Theoretical PhysicsETH Zurich
1909-1911Associate Professor of Theoretical PhysicsUniversity of Zurich
1902-1909Technical Expert, Class IIISwiss Patent Office, Bern

Honors and Awards

1921Nobel Prize in Physics, Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences
1925Copley Medal, Royal Society
1929Max Planck Medal, German Physical Society
1921Matteucci Medal, Italian Society of Sciences
1920Barnard Medal for Meritorious Service to Science, Columbia University

Memberships

Prussian Academy of Sciences, 1914-1933
Royal Society (Foreign Member), 1921-1955
National Academy of Sciences (USA), 1942-1955
Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences, 1920-1955

Bibliography and Products of Scholarship

* indicates a UNC or JHSPH student or trainee; ** indicates an GIDTRP student or trainee; † indicates corresponding authorship; ‡ indicates equal contribution

Peer-Reviewed Publications (10 total)

  1. Einstein A, Bose SN (1924) Quantentheorie des einatomigen idealen Gases. Sitzungsberichte der Preussischen Akademie der Wissenschaften. 1:261-267
  2. Einstein A, Rosen N (1935) The Particle Problem in the General Theory of Relativity. Physical Review. 48:73-77
    doi:10.1103/PhysRev.48.73
  3. Einstein A, de Haas WJ (1915) Experimental Proof of the Existence of Ampere's Molecular Currents. Proceedings of the KNAW. 18:696-711
  4. Einstein A, Infeld L, Hoffmann B (1938) The Gravitational Equations and the Problem of Motion. Annals of Mathematics. 39(1):65-100
  5. Einstein A (1905) Ist die Tragheit eines Korpers von seinem Energieinhalt abhangig?. Annalen der Physik. 323(13):639-641
    doi:10.1002/andp.19053231314
  6. Einstein A (1905) Uber einen die Erzeugung und Verwandlung des Lichtes betreffenden heuristischen Gesichtspunkt. Annalen der Physik. 322(6):132-148
    doi:10.1002/andp.19053220607
  7. Einstein A (1905) Uber die von der molekularkinetischen Theorie der Warme geforderte Bewegung von in ruhenden Flussigkeiten suspendierten Teilchen. Annalen der Physik. 322(8):549-560
    doi:10.1002/andp.19053220806
  8. Einstein A (1905) Zur Elektrodynamik bewegter Korper. Annalen der Physik. 322(10):891-921
    doi:10.1002/andp.19053221004
  9. Einstein A (1916) Die Grundlage der allgemeinen Relativitatstheorie. Annalen der Physik. 354(7):769-822
    doi:10.1002/andp.19163540702
  10. Einstein A, Podolsky B, Rosen N (1935) Can Quantum-Mechanical Description of Physical Reality Be Considered Complete?. Physical Review. 47(10):777-780
    doi:10.1103/PhysRev.47.777

Patents (1 total)

  1. Refrigerator with no moving parts (Einstein-Szilard refrigerator) (No. US1781541). Einstein A, Szilard L. granted

Published Articles and Editorials not Peer Reviewed (0 total)

Books & Chapters (2 total)

  1. Einstein A, Infeld L (1938) The Evolution of Physics. , Cambridge University Press.
  2. Einstein A (1922) The Meaning of Relativity. , Princeton University Press.

Preprints (0 total)

Letters (2 total)

  1. Einstein A (1949) Reply to Criticisms (in Albert Einstein Philosopher-Scientist). Library of Living Philosophers.
  2. Einstein A (1950) Letter to the Editor on Unified Field Theory. Physical Review.

Posters and Presentations at Scientific Meetings (3 total)

  1. Einstein A (1920) Ether and the Theory of Relativity. University of Leiden Lecture. (Invited)
  2. Einstein A (1916) On the Quantum Theory of Radiation. Zurich Physical Society. (oral)
  3. Einstein A (1913) On the Present Status of the Problem of Gravitation. German Natural Scientists and Physicians. (oral)
